title: quercus garryana var. fruticosa
intro: Quercus garryana var. fruticosa, also known as the Garry oak scrub or dwarf oak, is a variety of the Garry oak tree, a deciduous shrub native to the Pacific Northwest region of North America. It is used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments due to its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which help to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory problems. The health benefits of Quercus garryana var. fruticosa include its ability to support immune function, reduce inflammation, and promote wound healing, making it a valuable herb for overall well-being. Herbal preparations of Quercus garryana var. fruticosa can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and reap its health benefits.
